summary: Good matrix mix captures an energetic late‑80s show with standout performances of Sugaree, Let It Grow and a fun Throwing Stones → Lovelight transition.
review: The recording is a well‑remastered matrix (about half audience, half soundboard) that sounds clear enough for most listeners, though some note a slight audience bias. Musically the concert is upbeat and tight – the first set shines with Sugaree, West L.A., Let It Grow and Don't Ease, while the second set kicks off strong with an Iko jam and a lively Throwing Stones → Lovelight segue. A few moments (Black Muddy River encore and a rushed Morning Dew) draw criticism, but overall it’s an enjoyable late‑80s Dead show.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and deem the matrix recording good; a few negatives lower the average but not enough to drop the rating below strong‑good.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered matrix sounds roughly 50% audience, 50% soundboard; clear overall with some reviewers noting a slight audience dominance but no major dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is upbeat and rollicking with strong song choices; second set opens with a punchy Iko jam and maintains momentum through a fun Throwing Stones → Lovelight transition, though the encore drags a bit.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Awesome guitar work noted; vocal tone a little harsh/worn later in the night.
